data.description:

data.title: No room for Huawei and China in Germany’s future 6G networks
data.description:
German Chancellor Friedrich Merz, in a bid to assert Germany’s digital sovereignty, said the country won’t allow Chinese components in its 6G network. Merz’s announcement is the latest in a growing line of European countries dumping Chinese tech and cutting ties with manufacturers they evaluate to be risks. Germany’s digital sovereignty and national security concerns […]
source: https://www.cryptopolitan.com/no-huawei-china-in-germany-6g-networks/

data.title: dYdX approves proposal to triple buyback rate from 25% to 75%
data.description:
The dYdX platform has approved a major governance proposal to dramatically increase the allocation of net protocol revenue and buy back DYDX tokens, shifting from the previous 25% to a new 75%. The updated buyback allocation forms part of a broader strategy to enhance value capture, staking yield, and governance participation, directly linking tokenholder incentives […]

data.title: VanEck Submits Form 8-A for Solana Spot ETF to SEC
data.description: According to PANews, VanEck, a prominent ETF issuer, has submitted Form 8-A to the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) for its Solana spot ETF. This filing typically indicates that the product is nearing its launch. The submission follows the S-1 form filed at the end of October.

data.title: XRP ETF Launch Breaks Records as Canary Capital’s XRPC Surpasses Solana ETF on Day One
data.description:
Canary Capital’s newly launched spot XRP exchange-traded fund (ETF), trading under the ticker XRPC, delivered one of the strongest ETF debuts of the year, and the biggest launch so far for a non-Bitcoin, non-Ethereum crypto fund.
source: https://www.ethnews.com/xrp-etf-launch-breaks-records-as-canary-capitals-xrpc-surpasses-solana-etf-on-day-one/

data.title: Cash App Expands Crypto Capabilities With Lightning Payments and Stablecoin Support
data.description:
Cash App is taking a major step beyond its long-standing identity as a bitcoin-first platform by rolling out new features that enhance both speed and flexibility for millions of users. The company has introduced Lightning Network payments for eligible accounts, along with upcoming support for sending and receiving stablecoins.
source: https://www.ethnews.com/cash-app-expands-crypto-capabilities-with-lightning-payments-and-stablecoin-support/

data.title: Crypto Treasuries Turn Defensive as Solana Upexi’s Buyback Adds to Growing DAT Trend
data.description:
Upexi joins a growing trend among defensive crypto treasuries, with its board approving a $50 million share repurchase as its stock price declines and its market cap falls below its asset value.

data.title: Minneapolis Fed President Kashkari Expresses Uncertainty Over December Rate Decision
data.description:
According to PANews, Minneapolis Federal Reserve President Neel Kashkari has expressed uncertainty regarding the best course of action for the upcoming December meeting, despite not supporting the Fed's previous rate cut decision. On Thursday, Kashkari noted that the resilience of economic activity appears stronger than anticipated based on scattered evidence and data. He suggested that the Fed should have paused rate cuts during the October meeting, as subsequent data indicates that the economic situation has remained largely unchanged.

Regarding the December rate decision, Kashkari stated, "I can make a case for a rate cut based on the direction of the data, or I can argue for maintaining the current rates. We will have to wait and see." Following the September meeting, Kashkari had predicted two more rate cuts in 2025, citing a more pronounced economic slowdown at the time.

He highlighted reports of low-income borrowers facing difficulties, indicating some vulnerabilities within the labor market. However, he also noted that many companies are performing well financially and hold optimistic views for 2026.

data.title: FDIC Develops Guidelines for Tokenized Deposit Insurance
data.description:
According to PANews, the U.S. Federal Deposit Insurance Corporation (FDIC) is formulating guidelines for tokenized deposit insurance to assist financial institutions in expanding their digital asset operations. Acting Chairman Travis Hill emphasized that the transition of deposits from the traditional financial sector to blockchain or distributed ledger systems should not alter their legal status.

Hill's remarks come amid ongoing debates about how fintech companies, which are not directly insured by the FDIC, should fully compensate consumers if their funds are compromised. Although many fintech firms collaborate with FDIC-insured banks to offer products covered by 'pass-through deposit insurance,' this protection may be challenged if a third-party partner goes bankrupt, potentially jeopardizing consumer rights. The U.S. government's deposit insurance fund serves as a cornerstone of the financial system, designed to safeguard depositors in the event of bank failures.

data.title: Fed Official Comments on Dollar's Decline and Inflation Concerns
data.description:
According to PANews, Federal Reserve official Hamark stated that while the U.S. dollar is not a central topic in discussions, its weakening this year is not a cause for concern. Hamark explained that the dollar's decline is largely a correction from its previously strong position, bringing it closer to a fair valuation compared to other currencies.

Hamark emphasized the importance of maintaining restrictive interest rate policies to exert downward pressure on inflation, which remains a concern. She noted that the Federal Reserve faces challenges in balancing its dual mandate of managing inflation and employment. "Overall, I believe we need to maintain a degree of tightening to continue pressuring inflation back to our target level," she added.
